metadata.description: {\rtf1\ansi\deff0{\fonttbl{\f0\fnil Sans-Serif;}} \fs22 Michael Briggs is an award-winning audio engineer and producer based in Denton, Texas, available for production, mixing, recording, and mastering. He has worked with over 400 artists on more than 3,000 songs across just about every genre. Voted Best Producer and Best Audio Engineer in North Texas.\par \par \b Links\b0\par \par - Website: https://michaelbriggs.audio\par - Mastering: https://michaelbriggsmastering.com\par - GitHub: https://github.com/michaelbriggsaudio/mbriggs-reaper\par }

reapack.name: ReaAssist.lua
reapack.desc: ReaAssist - The Session-Aware Workflow Assistant for REAPER

metadata.description: {\rtf1\ansi\deff0{\fonttbl{\f0\fnil Sans-Serif;}} \fs22 ReaAssist is a session-aware workflow assistant for REAPER. Describe what you want done in plain English and ReaAssist reads your project state, then generates the Lua script, JSFX plugin, plugin parameter changes, or other automation to do it. Press send and the code runs in REAPER, with built-in safety checks for risky patterns.\par \par \b What It Does\b0\par \par - Reads your project state (tracks, items, FX chains, plugin parameters, transport, time selection, edit cursor, routing) so requests like "compress the vocal" act on the right target.\par - Configures plugins by name and parameter, dialing in precise dB / Hz / ms / % values. Includes verified parameter references for REAPER stock plugins (ReaEQ, ReaComp, ReaXcomp, ReaGate, etc.) and many popular third-party plugins (FabFilter Pro-Q 4, Pro-C 3, Pro-L 2, Soundtoys Decapitator, EchoBoy, etc.). Aliases auto-resolved.\par - Generates Lua scripts on demand: track and item creation, FX configuration, MIDI editing, marker and region placement, theme changes, and anything else the REAPER ReaScript API supports.\par - Writes JSFX (custom DSP code) on explicit request when stock plugins do not cover a specific need. Opt-in only; generic effect requests route through stock or third-party plugin workflows.\par - Surfaces and fixes mistakes when you tell it what went wrong.\par \par \b What It Is Not\b0\par \par ReaAssist is a workflow and automation tool, not a generative creative tool. It does not produce audio, music, samples, lyrics, melodies, or any other creative content. It does not make artistic decisions for you. Its job is to translate workflow steps you already know you want into the script code or plugin configuration that performs them, faster than you would write the code yourself.\par \par \b Privacy And Local Models\b0\par \par In addition to cloud providers, ReaAssist supports custom provider endpoints and local models running on your own machine via OpenAI-compatible servers (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, and similar). Point ReaAssist at a localhost endpoint and your project context, prompts, and responses never leave your computer. Useful when you cannot or prefer not to send session data to a cloud provider, or when you want to keep working offline.\par \par \b Requirements\b0\par \par - REAPER 7.0 or later\par - ReaImGui extension (install via ReaPack from the ReaTeam Extensions repository)\par - An API key from one of the supported cloud providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google), or a local / custom-endpoint setup as described above\par \par \b Getting Started\b0\par \par After installing, run the action "Script: ReaAssist.lua" from REAPER's Action List. Recommended: bind it to a toolbar button or keyboard shortcut. The first launch opens a Settings panel where you enter your provider details and pick your default model. From there, click "+ new chat" and describe what you want done.\par \par \b Links\b0\par \par - Website: https://reaassist.app\par - GitHub: https://github.com/michaelbriggsaudio/mbriggs-reaper\par }

version.author: Michael Briggs
version.changelog: - Curated FabFilter and ReEQ-family plugins now auto-attach their verified parameter reference when set as your preferred plugin - Non-linear curve params (Pro-C 3 Release, Pro-G timing, etc.) land at the exact requested dB/ms/Hz instead of an approximation - D_VOL volume reads use the correct linear-amplitude conversion - Anthropic flagship updated: Opus 4.6 -> Opus 4.7 (same price tier) - Defer-compliance check catches param writes outside reaper.defer() that some VST3 plugins silently ignore - Defer validator no longer false-positives on locally-defined helper functions called from inside a defer block - Empty-response auto-retry when reasoning consumes the entire output budget - Test API Keys defers when curl is busy and uses honest error wording (no more false "internet down") - Gemini tier detection: ambiguous responses no longer poison the cached tier; auto-retests once on next launch - Plugin bundle data tables moved to named-field schema to prevent the "completed OK then deferred crash" failure mode - Max Tokens dropdown removed; each model uses its published output ceiling automatically - "API Calls" counter always visible in the Details box; Context label correctly reflects when docs were pinned - Removing the active provider's API key in Settings no longer leaves the home screen with Send disabled - Larger Delete Provider confirmation popup - Friendly error message if Resources/UI.lua is launched directly - Adaptive cache TTL: 5 min on first turn, escalating to 1 hour once multi-turn intent is confirmed - Plugin and API-reference bundles split into core + on-demand sections - Docs core co-pinned alongside plugin context on chain-phrase preempts - Single-word / conversational prompts no longer trigger a docs fetch round-trip - Curated user-prefs route through plugin_ref instead of live-scan - Track-iteration paths nil-guard against project-tab-close races - Hot-path optimizations from log analysis (cached field maps, throttled session-cache rebuilds) - Resources/ filenames consolidated and de-prefixed

version.author: Michael Briggs
version.changelog: - Plain-English chat interface for REAPER automation - Reads project state and acts on it directly: plugin parameters, FX chains, routing - Lua and JSFX generation with built-in safety scanner - Provider support: Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, plus local/custom OpenAI-compatible endpoints - Per-message Undo, optional pre-run session backups, dangerous-code review modal
